sections.label: Section 840
sections.original: ὦ Θάνατε , Θάνατε , νῦν μ ’ ἐπίσκεψαι μολών !
sections.translation: And may they seize those wicked men with most wicked destruction , just as they see me fall slain by my own hand , so slain by their own kin may they perish at the hand of their best-loved offspring . Come , you swift and punishing Erinyes , devour all the assembled army and spare nothing !

sections.label: Section 845
sections.original: ὦ φέγγος , ὦ γῆς ἱερὸν οἰκείας πέδον
sections.translation: And you , Helios , whose chariot-wheels climb the steep sky , when you see the land of my fathers , draw in your rein spread with gold and tell my disasters and my fate to my aged father and to the unhappy woman who nursed me .

sections.label: Section 850
sections.original: τοῦθ ’ ὑμὶν Αἴας τοὖπος ὕστατον θροεῖ ·
sections.translation: Poor mother ! Indeed , I think , when she hears this news , she will sing a song of loud wailing throughout the entire city . But it is not for me to weep in vain like this . No , the deed must quickly have its beginning . O Death , Death , come now and lay your eyes on me !

sections.label: Section 855
sections.translation: And yet I will meet you also in that other world and there address you . But you , beam of the present bright day , I salute you and the Sun in his chariot for the last time and never again . O light ! O sacred soil

sections.label: Section 860
sections.translation: of my own Salamis , firm seat of my father ’ s hearth ! O famous Athens , and your race kindred to mine ! And you , springs and rivers of this land — and you plains of Troy I salute you also — farewell , you who have nurtured me ! This is the last word that Ajax speaks to you .

sections.label: Section 865
sections.original: Τίς ἂν δῆτά μοι , τίς ἂν φιλοπόνων
sections.translation: The rest he will tell to the shades in Hades .

sections.label: Section 866
sections.translation: Toil follows toil yielding toil ! Where , where have I not trudged ? And still no place can say that I have shared its secret .

sections.label: Section 870
sections.translation: Listen ! A sudden thud !

sections.label: Section 872
sections.translation: We made it , we shipmates of your voyage .

sections.label: Section 873
sections.original:
890
ἀλλ ’ ἀμενηνὸν ἄνδρα μὴ λεύσσειν ὅπου .
sections.translation: What news , then ?

sections.label: Section 874
sections.translation: All the westward flank of the ships has been scoured for tracks .

sections.label: Section 875
sections.translation: And did you find anything ?

sections.label: Section 876
sections.translation: Only an abundance of toil . There was nothing more to see .

sections.label: Section 877
sections.translation: Neither , as a matter of fact , has the man been seen along the path that faces the shafts of the morning sun .

sections.label: Section 879
sections.translation: Who , then , can guide me ? What toiling

sections.label: Section 880
sections.translation: fisherman , busy about his sleepless hunt , what nymph of the Olympian heights or of the streams that flow toward

sections.label: Section 885
sections.translation: Bosporus , can say whether she has anywhere seen the wanderings of fierce-hearted Ajax ? It is cruel that I , who have roamed with such great toil , cannot come near him with a fair course ,

sections.label: Section 890
sections.translation: but fail to see where the enfeebled man is .

sections.label: Section 891
sections.original: Ὤ μοι ἐμᾶς ἄτας , οἶος ἄρ ’ αἱμάχθης ,
sections.translation: Ah , me , ah , me !

sections.label: Section 892
sections.translation: Whose cry broke from that nearby grove ?

sections.label: Section 893
sections.original: ἐγὼ δ ’ ὁ πάντα κωφὸς , ὁ πάντ ’ ἄϊδρις ,
sections.translation: Ah , misery !

sections.label: Section 894
sections.translation: There , I see his unfortunate young bride , who was the prize of his spear ,

sections.label: Section 895
sections.translation: Tecmessa , dissolved in that pitiful wailing .

sections.label: Section 896
sections.original:
915
Οὔ τοι θεατός · ἀλλά νιν περιπτυχεῖ
sections.translation: I am lost , destroyed , razed to the ground , my friends !

sections.label: Section 897
sections.translation: What is it ?

sections.label: Section 898
sections.original: οὐδεὶς ἂν ὅστις καὶ φίλος τλαίη βλέπειν
sections.translation: Here is our Ajax — his blood newly shed , he lies folded around the sword , burying it .

sections.label: Section 900
sections.original: πληγῆς μελανθὲν αἷμ ’ ἀπ ’ οἰκείας σφαγῆς .
sections.translation: Ah , no ! Our homecoming is lost ! Ah , my king , you have killed me , the comrade of your voyage ! Unhappy man — broken-hearted woman !

sections.label: Section 904
sections.original: ὦ ὅύςμορ ’ Αἴας , οἶος ὢν οἴως ἔχεις ,
sections.translation: His condition demands that we cry aiai .

sections.label: Section 905
sections.original: ὡς καὶ παρ ’ ἐχθροῖς ἄξιος θρήνων τυχεῖν .
sections.translation: But by whose hand can the ill-fated man have contrived this end ?

sections.label: Section 906
sections.translation: He did it with his own hand ; it is obvious . This sword which he planted in the ground and on which he fell convicts him .

sections.label: Section 908
sections.translation: Ah , what blind folly I have displayed ! All alone , then , you bled , unguarded by your friends ! And I took no care , so entirely dull was I , so totally stupid . Where , where lies inflexible Ajax , whose name means anguish ?

sections.label: Section 915
sections.translation: No , he is not to be looked at ! I will cover him over entirely with this enfolding shroud , since no one — no one , that is , who loves him — could bear to see him spurt the darkened gore of his self-inflicted slaughter up his nostrils and out of the bloody gash .

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: Σοὶ μὲν δοκεῖν ταῦτ ’ ἔστ ’, ἐμοὶ δ ’ ἄγαν φρονεῖν .
sections.translation: Ah , what shall I do ? What loved one is there to lift you in his arms ? Where is Teucer ? How timely would be his arrival , if he would but come to compose the corpse of his brother here ! Ah , unlucky Ajax , from so great a height you are fallen so low !

sections.label: Section 924
sections.translation: Even among your enemies you are worthy of mourning !

sections.label: Section 925
sections.translation: You were bound , poor man , with that unbending heart you were bound , it seems , to fulfill a harsh destiny of limitless toils ! So wild to my ears

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: Τοιόνδε μέντοι Ζῆνός ἡ δεινὴ θεὸς
sections.translation: were the words of hatred which in your fierce mood you moaned against the Atreidae with such deadly passion . True it is that that moment was a potent source of sorrows ,

sections.label: Section 935
sections.translation: when the arms were made the prize for a contest in the skills of warfare !

sections.label: Section 937
sections.translation: Ah ! Ah !

sections.label: Section 938
sections.original: τοῖς τοῦδ ’· ἴσως τοι , κεἰ βλέποντα μὴ ’ πόθουν ,
sections.translation: True anguish , I know , pierces your heart .

sections.label: Section 939
sections.original: θανόντ ’ ἂν οἰμώξειαν ὲν χρείᾳ δορός .
sections.translation: Ah ! Ah , me !

sections.label: Section 940
sections.original: οἱ γὰρ κακοὶ γνώμαισι τἀγαθὸν χεροῖν
sections.translation: I do not wonder , lady , that you wail and wail again , when you have just lost one so loved .

sections.label: Section 942
sections.original: ἐμοὶ πικρὸς τέθνηκεν ἢ κείνοις γλυκὺς ,
sections.translation: It is for you to analyze my troubles , but for me to feel them too fully .

sections.label: Section 943
sections.original: αὑτῷ δὲ τερπνός · ὧν γὰρ ἠράσθη τυχεῖν ,
sections.translation: I must agree .

sections.label: Section 944
sections.translation: Oh , my son , to what a heavy yoke of slavery

sections.label: Section 945
sections.original: πῶς δῆτα τοῦδ ’ ἐπεγγελῷεν ἂν κάτα ;
sections.translation: we advance ! What cruel task-masters stand over us !

sections.label: Section 946
sections.original:
970
θεοῖς τέθνηκεν οὕτος , οὐ κείνοισιν , οὔ .
sections.translation: Ah , the deeds of the two ruthless Atreidae which you name in our present grief would be unthinkable ! May the gods hold them back !

sections.label: Section 950
sections.translation: These events that you see would not have happened as they have without the will of the gods .

sections.label: Section 951
sections.original:
975
Σίγησον · αὐδὴν γὰρ δοκῶ Τεύκρου κλύειν
sections.translation: Yes , they have brought upon us a burden too heavy to bear .

sections.label: Section 952
sections.translation: Yet what suffering the divine daughter of Zeus , fierce Pallas , engenders for Odysseus ’ sake !

sections.label: Section 955
sections.translation: No doubt the much-enduring hero exults in his dark soul and mocks in loud laughter at these frenzied sorrows — what shame !—

sections.label: Section 960
sections.original: τὸ τοῦδε , ποῦ μοι γῆς κυρεῖ τῆς Τρῳάδος ;
sections.translation: and with him , when they hear the news , will laugh the royal brothers , the Atreidae .

sections.label: Section 961
sections.translation: Then let them mock and rejoice at this man ’ s misery . Perhaps , even though they did not cherish him while he lived , they will lament his death , when they meet with the difficulties of war . Men of crooked judgment do not know what good

sections.label: Section 965
sections.translation: they have in their hands until they have thrown it away . His death is more bitter to me than it is sweet to the Greeks ; but in any case to Ajax himself it is a joy , since he has accomplished all that he desired to get — his longed-for death . So why should they exult over him ?

sections.label: Section 970
sections.original: ὁδός θ ’ ὁδῶν πασῶν ἀνιάσασα δὴ
sections.translation: He died before the gods , not at all before them — no ! And so let Odysseus toss his insults in empty glee . For them Ajax is no more ; for me he is gone , abandoning me to anguish and mourning .

sections.label: Section 974
sections.original: ὀξεῖα γάρ σου βάξις , ὡς θεοῦ τινος ,
sections.translation: Ah ! Ah , no !

sections.label: Section 975
sections.original: διῆλθ ’ Ἀχαιοὺς πάντας , ὡς οἴχει θανὼν ,
sections.translation: Quiet — I think I hear the voice of Teucer striking a note that points to this disaster .

sections.label: Section 977
sections.translation: Beloved Ajax , brother whose face was so dear to me , have you truly fared as the mighty rumor says ?

sections.label: Section 979
sections.original: ἴθ ’, ἐκκάλυψον , ὡς ἴδω τὸ πᾶν κακόν .
sections.translation: He is dead , Teucer . Take it as fact .

sections.label: Section 980
sections.original: ὦ δυςθέατον ὄμμα καὶ τόλμης πικρὰς ,
sections.translation: Then I am destroyed by my heavy fortune !

sections.label: Section 981
sections.translation: When things stand as they do —
